mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and encourages a reach-for-yield rotation into equities, producing persistently positive S&P 500 forward returns as long as the policy rate remains near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15 percent, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0%, indicating a labor market deep in slack following a recession, subsequent stabilization or marginal improvement in employment conditions triggers a mean-reversion equity rally over the following quarter as markets price in eventual recovery.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.0% and PAYEMS month-over-month change turns non-negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8 percent, elevated post-recession level
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 9.0% triggers are negative in more than 50% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales growth and housing starts signals a joint contraction in consumer and residential demand that is not visible from either series alone; this combined demand deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598 thousand units, historically depressed level
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS 3-month change x HOUST 3-month change) shows Rank IC < 0.03 with forward 20d returns over any 2-year rolling window, or joint-decline episodes do not precede negative returns in majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, a sustained rise in industrial production reflects re-acceleration of manufacturing activity that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; the same signal is uninformative or reversed during late-cycle expansions when capacity constraints dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.44, still below prior peak indicating recovery phase
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 60d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during periods identified as post-recession recovery, or sign flips relative to hypothesis in over 40% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in nonfarm payroll employment growth is immediately absorbed into equity prices; a month-over-month increase in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week as it signals near-term earnings resilience before slower-moving data confirms the trend.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or sign of PAYEMS change fails to match sign of subsequent 5d return in majority of months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while headline CPI accelerates, markets anticipate an eventual policy tightening cycle; this joint condition of ultra-low rates plus rising inflation pressure predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as valuation multiples begin to price in future rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2%
regime_hint: zero rate policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Instances where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPI YoY > 2% do not show negative 20d forward returns in majority of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 across the joint condition sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 600 thousand units annualized signals continued residential investment weakness that historically precedes broader consumer retrenchment, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter until starts recover above this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598.0 thousand units, below 600k threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 600k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of elevated unemployment above 9%, industrial production gains are interpreted by markets as genuine capacity utilization recovery rather than late-cycle overheating, so INDPRO increases predict positive S&P 500 forward returns; in low-unemployment regimes the same INDPRO increase signals capacity strain and predicts flat or negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8 percent alongside INDPRO at 97.44
failure_condition: Interaction of INDPRO momentum with UNRATE > 9% regime fails to produce Rank IC >= 0.03 with forward 20d returns over any 2-year rolling window, or direction flips relative to hypothesis in majority of high-unemployment episodes.
